commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a generated `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, release/package validators, dependency-review labeling/CODEOWNERS coverage, tests, docs, and changelog text for npm shrinkwrap handling.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection and `git merge-tree` show the PR shrinkwrap was generated from older dependency versions than current main and that the branch conflicts in `CHANGELOG.md`.
commands.review_followup: This release/security-owned PR has concrete blockers, but final cutoff policy and owner approval should stay with human release/secops reviewers rather than an automatic repair lane.

target.title: chore: harden npm shrinkwrap release path
target.body:
## Summary
- Ship the OpenClaw npm package with a generated `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, produced from root publish dependencies plus `pnpm-workspace.yaml` override policy.
- Add shrinkwrap and `package-lock.json` to the dependency security review surface through CODEOWNERS, dependency-change awareness, labeler sizing, and dependency report output.
- Enforce shrinkwrap in release/package validators while allowing already-published packages through `2026.5.20` to pass with a legacy warning.
- Document shrinkwrap support on the gateway security page and add a changelog entry.

## Explainer
`npm-shrinkwrap.json` is npm's publishable lockfile. Unlike `package-lock.json`, npm includes it in published packages, so installing `openclaw` from npm can resolve through the same audited dependency graph that release tooling generated.

This does not replace `pnpm-lock.yaml` for source development. The repo still uses pnpm as the source-of-truth package manager, and the shrinkwrap generator derives the published npm lock from the package's runtime dependencies while applying workspace-level override policy. That keeps release installs more deterministic without making shrinkwrap a sandbox or vulnerability scanner.

Because lockfiles can redirect resolved package versions and tarballs, this PR treats both `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and `package-lock.json` as security-review surfaces. The release path rejects accidental `package-lock.json` in the npm package, requires shrinkwrap for new packages, and checks that the generated shrinkwrap has no dev dependency graph.

## Verification
- `node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --check`
- `NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/tmp/openclaw-npm-cache npm pack --dry-run --ignore-scripts --json`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-changes-report.test.ts test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts test/openclaw-npm-release-check.test.ts test/package-scripts.test.ts test/package-manager-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `actionlint .github/workflows/dependency-change-awareness.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml`
- `npm pack openclaw@2026.4.25 --json --pack-destination <tmp> && node scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.mjs <tmp>/*.tgz`
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --uncommitted`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OpenClaw npm package installs now carry a publishable shrinkwrap, and lockfile/shrinkwrap changes are treated as security-review surfaces.

Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout with npm pack dry-run, the current generated shrinkwrap, focused Vitest coverage, docs checks, workflow linting, and a real published `openclaw@2026.4.25` tarball compatibility check.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Generated shrinkwrap check, dry-run npm pack, focused tests for release/tarball/dependency-review surfaces, docs check, actionlint, legacy published tarball validation, and Codex autoreview.

Evidence after fix: Dry-run pack includes `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and excludes `package-lock.json`; generated shrinkwrap has 414 packages, 0 dev packages, and selected workspace overrides match `pnpm-workspace.yaml`.

Observed result after fix: Release/package checks and focused tests pass; the legacy published tarball emits a legacy shrinkwrap warning and passes; docs check passes; autoreview found no actionable correctness issues.

What was not tested: Full release CI, broad `pnpm check`, and an actual npm publish.
target.author: steipete
